response.justification: Mohammed Al-Said is a skilled full-stack and systems developer with a broad polyglot background, but he is fundamentally mismatched for this role. The position mandates 8 years of C++ experience — C++ does not even appear in his listed skills, and his only verifiable C/C++ work is a 6-month stint at National Instruments (2014–2015) plus a bachelor thesis project. His career has been dominated by TypeScript, Node.js, DLang, and web-layer work. While he has touched distributed systems (Sociomantic/Dunnhumby) and cloud tooling (AWS CDK, Kubernetes), this is application-level exposure rather than the deep cloud compute platform infrastructure the role requires. He also lacks a formal technical leadership track record commensurate with a mid-senior tech lead position.
strengths: Demonstrated experience with high-throughput distributed systems (millions of HTTP req/s at Sociomantic) and event-driven microservice architectures
Strong testing culture and TDD advocacy backed by workshops, open-source tooling (Fluent-Asserts, Trial), and consistent application across multiple employers
Broad infrastructure and DevOps exposure including Kubernetes, Docker, CI/CD pipelines, and AWS CDK, showing platform-adjacent awareness
concerns: C++ is absent from his skills list and his total C/C++ work amounts to roughly 6 months — against a hard requirement of 8 years
No evidence of leading or mentoring a cloud or DevOps engineering team; 'Staff' titles reflect individual contributor seniority, not people/tech leadership at scale
Primary technical domain is web and application development (TypeScript, Node.js, DLang), not cloud compute infrastructure, storage systems, or hardware architecture
